session_hint: the JWT token validation failure investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing intermittent JWT token validation failures in the auth service?
turns: About 0.5% of authenticated API requests are returning 401 Unauthorized. The tokens are freshly issued — some fail validation within seconds of creation. Running auth-service v2.3 on three EC2 instances.
Checked the JWT validation error logs: the error is 'token is not valid yet'. The nbf (not before) claim check is failing. Our token-service sets nbf to the exact current time at issuance.

Compared system clocks: token-service is 87 seconds ahead of auth-service-2. When auth-service-2 validates a token issued by token-service, its local time is still before the token's nbf, so it rejects the token as not yet valid.

The NTP daemon on auth-service-2 had crashed and the clock drifted over several days. Restarted NTP, clock re-synced, failures stopped. Root cause: NTP clock drift on auth-service-2 causing nbf validation to reject freshly issued tokens.
